chief_complaint: An 85-year-old Japanese male presented with urinary incontinence, gait disturbance, and cognitive decline.
history: The patient had a history of diabetes, hyperlipidemia, and a pulmonary vein isolation procedure for atrial fibrillation. He was diagnosed with normal-pressure hydrocephalus (NPH) and underwent lumbar-peritoneal shunt surgery. Postoperatively, he developed pneumonia followed by an intra-abdominal abscess and bacterial meningitis.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Corynebacterium striatum was isolated from both cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) and intraperitoneal abscess. Blood cultures and other investigations were not mentioned.
correct_diagnosis: Lumbar-peritoneal shunt-associated meningitis and peritoneal abscess due to Corynebacterium striatum
differentials: Lumbar-peritoneal shunt-associated meningitis and peritoneal abscess due to Corynebacterium striatum
Bacterial meningitis due to unknown cause
Intra-abdominal abscess due to Corynebacterium striatum
Septic shock due to Corynebacterium striatum
correct_ranking: Lumbar-peritoneal shunt-associated meningitis and peritoneal abscess due to Corynebacterium striatum
Bacterial meningitis due to unknown cause
Intra-abdominal abscess due to Corynebacterium striatum
